ranges.notes: Cold and often wet weather, quiet tourist scene post-holiday; some local festivities around New Year and Epiphany.
Cold, damp winter weather continues; limited outdoor activities; low tourist traffic.
Typical winter weather with short daylight; museums and indoor attractions recommended; low crowds.
Weather remains cold but some clear days possible; quieter city, good for budget travelers and cultural visits.
Winter continues with cold, often wet weather but slightly longer daylight; fewer tourists—good for museums and indoor events.
Some romantic events start mid-month; cold but manageable weather; restaurants and theaters prepare for Valentine's Day.
Valentine’s Day events attract couples; cozy indoor venues popular; still cold but city ambiance is charming.
Late winter; weather still cold with occasional rain; fewer tourists, good value accommodation.
Early spring brings slightly warmer weather; rain still common; fewer tourists; good deals on hotels.

ranges.event: St Patrick’s Day (17th March - local celebrations)
ranges.notes: Some Irish-themed celebrations in pubs; weather improving slightly; moderate visitor interest.
Mid-March sees better weather and longer days; outdoor walks along the Quayside become more pleasant; moderate crowds.
Spring bloom begins; temperatures rise; good time for outdoor photography and sightseeing; fewer tourists than summer.
Spring weather improves with mild temperatures; gardens and parks start blooming; moderate crowds.
Easter period can bring more visitors depending on holiday timing; good for family visits and outdoor activities.

ranges.event: Easter Holidays (variable dates, mid-April some years)
ranges.notes: High family and leisure traveler interest; festive events and longer daylight hours; weather can vary between mild to rainy.
Late April is pleasant with blooming gardens; good for walking along the Tyne river; moderate visitor numbers.
Mild weather, longer daylight, and public holidays attract visitors; many outdoor festivals and events start.
Typically some of the best spring weather; great for exploring Newcastle’s historic sites and outdoor spaces; growing crowds.

ranges.event: Newcastle International Film Festival (usually mid-late May)
ranges.notes: Cultural events increase appeal; pleasant weather; popular among culture seekers and tourists.
Late May is warm and sunny often; ideal for outdoor dining and river walks; crowds begin to build ahead of summer.
Start of summer; warm, generally dry; outdoor events and nightlife vibrant; good for all traveler types.
Major cultural event attracting diverse crowds; vibrant atmosphere; warm weather enhances enjoyment.
Long daylight hours for sightseeing; mild to warm weather; ideal for outdoor activities and festivals.
Peak summer weather with warm temperatures; busy tourist season; book accommodation early.
Warm summer weather; vibrant outdoor events and nightlife; Newcastle Festival often starts in July.
Major city-wide arts and music festival; attracts large crowds; excellent cultural experiences; warm weather.
Peak summer continues; good for beach day trips nearby; busy but lively city atmosphere.
Warm and mostly dry; great for outdoor dining and river cruises; busy tourist season continues.
Cultural festival celebrating South Asian heritage; warm weather; busy but festive atmosphere.
High summer weather persists; great for sightseeing and day trips to nearby coastlines; busy with tourists.
Late August begins to quieten slightly; weather still warm but occasional rain; good deals start to appear.
End of summer with warm weather; fewer tourists than peak July; good for budget travelers and cultural visits.
Early autumn with mild weather; fewer tourists; good for cultural events and river walks.
Temperatures start to cool but still pleasant; local food festivals often take place; good crowd levels.

ranges.event: Newcastle Science Festival (mid September some years)
ranges.notes: Indoor events attract families and curious travelers; weather can be variable; moderate to low crowds.
Cooler, wetter weather begins; fewer tourists; good for indoor attractions and budget travel.
Autumn weather cool and often rainy; fewer tourists; ideal for indoor cultural sites and museums.
Some local Halloween festivities and themed tours; cooler weather limits outdoor activities; modest crowds.
Weather turns colder and wetter; fewer events; low tourist numbers; less attractive for outdoor sightseeing.
Late October is chilly and damp; indoor attractions best; low crowds but limited daylight.
Cold, damp and short days; city quieter; few events; good for budget travelers seeking deals.
Fireworks and bonfire celebrations bring local energy; weather cold but manageable; short daylight.
Increasingly cold and wet; indoor activities recommended; quiet tourist scene.
Approaching Christmas season; shopping and early festive events begin; weather cold and often wet.
Festive decorations up; markets begin; cold weather; moderate crowds especially on weekends.
Christmas markets in full swing; festive atmosphere; cold but enjoyable; popular with families and couples.
Pre-Christmas shopping rush; festive shows and concerts; cold weather; busy city center.
City quieter in evening; cold weather; many shops and attractions close early; festive spirit high.

ranges.event: Christmas Day and New Year’s Eve
ranges.notes: Christmas Day is very quiet with many businesses closed; Boxing Day sales and New Year's Eve events attract crowds; cold weather.
